phase-plan.purpose: Make the public site and customer portal operational by turning submissions, scheduling, support, estimates, invoices, and recurring-plan scheduling into managed Payload/CRM relationships with EngageBay as the default provider and HubSpot as a supported fallback.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: form-to-contact sync, first-party scheduling forms, customer support intake, call and quote intake handoff, customer portal estimates/invoices/schedule/account surfaces, recurring service-plan defaults, CRM provider abstraction, runtime provider selection, and quote summary sync into CRM deals. Out of scope: customer-visible quote editing, accounting sync, and bidirectional CRM ownership unless explicitly approved later.

file.description: Public-site conversion flow and content checklist.
Quote workflow and CRM handoff planning.
Contact/support request categories and submission mapping.
Customer portal schedule page for appointments, recurring plans, and schedule-change requests.
Customer-facing invoice records used by the portal.
Scheduled or requested customer jobs used by the portal and ops calendar.
Recurring maintenance-plan records and calculated subscription totals.
Payload quote records that store CRM deal-sync metadata and remain the estimate source for the customer portal.
Quote-side CRM sync hook for sent/accepted/lost quotes.
HubSpot contact/deal sync implementation used by the active CRM provider abstraction.
Draftable recurring-plan defaults for the customer portal and service-plan records.

task.description: Keep every public lead tied to the active CRM provider with clear sync status and task ownership, and move quote/contact/schedule/support capture onto a shared first-party form system.

Make scheduling part of the lead pipeline with a first-party form flow, shared validators/helpers, customer portal schedule requests/reschedules, recurring-plan cadence, and Payload-to-CRM write-back instead of an embedded vendor form.
Use Payload collections/globals for customer portal estimates, invoices, recurring plans, service appointments, and self-serve account/contact updates.

Mirror Payload quotes into CRM deals so follow-up and relationship management happen in the chosen provider. Current delivery focus: one-way HubSpot deal sync with quote-side CRM metadata and env-configured stage mapping.

Admin-only HubSpot REST reads for `/ops`: account health probe, tasks due on a selected calendar day (portal timezone), and open-deal amount sum for pipeline KPI when HubSpot is the active runtime provider. EngageBay does not receive ops-board parity in this delivery.

questions.question: v1 treats Payload quotes as the source of truth and pushes one-way into CRM deals. Write-back remains deferred until the outbound sync is stable.
How should new leads and synced deals be assigned to an owner in the active CRM provider for follow-up?
Is the current EngageBay 403 coming from trial API-call limits, the wrong key scope, or a throttling window that needs retry logic?
